seo.title: Azure AI Agent Service Guardrails for Recruitment/HR
seo.description: Compliance guide for Azure AI Agent Service agents in recruitment/hr. Meet EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144 requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Recruitment/HR teams using Azure AI Agent Service face heightened compliance risks due to strict regulations like EEOC guidelines, NYC Local Law 144, and the EU AI Act classifying hiring AI as high-risk. Without proper guardrails, agent actions like resume screening or candidate ranking can inadvertently discriminate based on protected characteristics, leading to lawsuits and fines up to $1,500 per violation. Vex provides enterprise-grade middleware to validate agent outputs against compliance policies before they impact hiring decisions.

complianceRequirements.description: AI screening tools must not discriminate based on protected characteristics (race, gender, age, disability) and require annual bias audits.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex Guardrail validates candidate rankings and screening outputs against EEOC-protected attributes extracted from resumes, blocking discriminatory patterns before decisions are made.

complianceRequirements.description: Employers must notify candidates when AI is used in hiring decisions and conduct annual bias audits on automated employment decision tools.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex automatically injects AI disclosure notices into candidate communications and generates audit-ready logs of all agent decisions with bias scoring.

complianceRequirements.description: Hiring AI systems require risk management, transparency, human oversight, and conformity assessments.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces human-in-the-loop approval for high-risk decisions, provides explainability reports, and maintains immutable audit trails for conformity assessments.

complianceRequirements.description: AI tools must provide reasonable accommodations and not screen out candidates with disabilities.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex flags potential ADA violations in screening criteria and ensures accessibility-compliant candidate communications.

industryRisks.name: Protected Attribute Proxy Discrimination
industryRisks.description: Agent uses proxies like 'cultural fit' or zip code that correlate with protected characteristics.
industryRisks.example: Azure AI Agent ranks candidates lower from certain zip codes that correlate with minority neighborhoods.

industryRisks.name: Outdated Salary/Experience Grounding
industryRisks.description: Bing Search grounding returns stale market data leading to unfair experience requirements.
industryRisks.example: Agent rejects candidates because 2022 salary benchmarks don't match current market rates.

industryRisks.name: Missing AI Disclosure Notices
industryRisks.description: Candidates not informed AI used in screening, violating NYC Local Law 144.
industryRisks.example: Automated rejection emails lack required 'AI used in decision' disclosure language.

industryRisks.name: Token Limit Resume Parsing Failures
industryRisks.description: Long resumes exceed agent thread limits, causing incomplete parsing and unfair screening.
industryRisks.example: Senior candidates with detailed career histories get truncated evaluation.

implementation.title: Vex Middleware for Azure AI Agent Pipeline
implementation.description: Install Vex as middleware in your Azure AI Projects pipeline to validate all resume screening outputs against EEOC/ADA policies.
implementation.code:
from azure.ai.projects import AgentSession
from vex_sdk import VexGuardrail

# Install: pip install azure-ai-projects vex-sdk
guardrail = VexGuardrail(api_key="your-vex-key")

def screened_candidates(session: AgentSession, resumes: list):
    raw_results = session.invoke("screen_candidates", {"resumes": resumes})
    
    # Vex validates against EEOC protected attributes
    validated = guardrail.validate(
        raw_results,
        policy="hr_recruitment",
        rules=[
            "no_proxy_discrimination",
            "eeoc_protected_attributes",
            "ada_accommodation"
        ]
    )
    
    if validated.status == "BLOCKED":
        raise ValueError(f"Compliance violation: {validated.issues}")
    
    return validated.safe_output

implementation.title: Bias Audit Logging for Annual Compliance
implementation.description: Automatically log all screening decisions for annual bias audits required by multiple regulations.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Azure AI Agent Service in Recruitment/HR: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: Global tech company TalentFlow deploys Azure AI Agent Service to screen 50,000 engineering resumes monthly across US and EU offices. The agent uses Bing grounding for salary benchmarks, file search for internal job descriptions, and GPT-4o for ranking candidates by 'cultural fit' and experience. HR teams notice higher rejection rates from certain demographic groups but lack visibility into why. Without compliance guardrails, the system processes 10,000 decisions monthly without EEOC validation, NYC Local Law 144 disclosures, or EU AI Act transparency logging. Legal teams worry about accumulating liability as candidate volume grows 30% quarterly.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After integrating Vex middleware, TalentFlow blocked 847 discriminatory decisions in first month (12% of total), automatically added AI disclosure to 100% of communications, and generated EU AI Act-compliant audit reports. Bias scores dropped from 0.42 to 0.18 across protected attributes. Vex's token limit handling prevented 23% of parsing failures on senior-level resumes. The company passed its first annual bias audit with zero findings and gained 15% more diverse candidate advancement through transparent, compliant screening.

cta.heading: Comply with Recruitment/HR Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Add Vex middleware to your Azure AI Agent Service pipeline today to eliminate hiring compliance risks.
